description: Running guaranteed background work on a raw thread or one-off coroutine that the OS discards when it kills the process.
body: Failure mode: deferrable, must-complete work (sync, upload) is started on a bare thread or an unscoped coroutine tied to the process, so when Android kills the backgrounded app the work is silently dropped and never resumes. Detection signals: background sync/upload that fails to complete when the app is backgrounded or the device sleeps; tasks lost after a reboot; no durable scheduler in use. Do not rely on the process surviving for guaranteed work. Safer fallback: enqueue the work in WorkManager with constraints and a backoff policy, make it idempotent and resumable, and verify it completes after the app is killed and across a reboot.
